parentQuestion: Homeostasis is the condition in which the body maintains ___.

scaffolds.q: The body works to maintain stable internal conditions despite changes in the external environment.
scaffolds.explain: Your body temperature, blood pH, and blood sugar levels stay relatively constant even when external conditions change dramatically. Step outside in freezing weather? Your core temperature stays at 37°C. Give this idea space to land:into this: this self-regulation is ESSENTIAL for survival. Lose it, and you die.

scaffolds.q: Homeostasis means 'staying the same' in Greek.
scaffolds.explain: The word homeostasis comes from Greek: 'homeo' (same/similar) and 'stasis' (standing/stable). It refers to maintaining stable internal conditions — the foundation of staying alive. Let's explore this together: now.
